problem_statement: A county emergency shelter began rejecting newly issued volunteer badges as not yet valid for roughly eight minutes, while the same badges worked immediately at the county's other shelters.

observations.statement: Denials began at 02:10:11, immediately after the Harbor shelter controller resumed service following planned power maintenance; the final pre-maintenance badge event at 01:52:43 was accepted.
observations.provenance: Door-controller audit export aligned to the maintenance work order and UPS event log.

observations.statement: A battery-backed GPS logger connected to the controller's maintenance port measured the controller 487 seconds behind UTC after startup; the offset was below one second before shutdown.
observations.provenance: Facilities technician export from an independently disciplined logger with a current calibration record.

observations.statement: A retained packet capture shows the shelter gateway accepting a signed synchronization reply advertising a negative 487-second offset at 02:09:48, before the first badge denial.
observations.provenance: Ring-buffer capture preserved by network operations before the source was withdrawn.

observations.statement: The same six badges were accepted at two other shelters during the Harbor failures, and all three sites held byte-identical badge certificates and revocation lists.
observations.provenance: Supervised cross-site badge check with certificate bundle hashes recorded.

observations.statement: Re-anchoring the Harbor controller to the county's verified source removed the 487-second offset and all six badges were then accepted without reissuing credentials or restarting the access service.
observations.provenance: Approved maintenance action observed jointly by facilities and shelter security.

observations.statement: A disconnected bench reader accepted a copied badge bundle under verified UTC, rejected it when fed the retained negative-offset announcement, and accepted it again after verified UTC was restored.
observations.provenance: Three-phase bench replay with door outputs electrically isolated.

observations.statement: Monotonic measurements for card read, certificate verification, and lock response remained within their normal ranges throughout the incident, despite the civil timestamp offset.
observations.provenance: Controller performance counters exported by the access-service monitor.

observations.statement: Controller software hashes, badge policy settings, network routes, and certificate files were unchanged from the previous successful week.
observations.provenance: Independent release attestation, network snapshot, and credential inventory.

observations.statement: For 24 hours after the faulty source was withdrawn, the Harbor offset stayed below 0.9 seconds and 1,842 badge events completed without a not-yet-valid rejection.
observations.provenance: Post-maintenance shelter access report signed by the duty manager.
